sources.name: Armed Conflict Location & Event Data
sources.notes: Event-level data on political violence and protest. Single most granular dataset for current conflict events.

sources.name: Uppsala Conflict Data Program
sources.notes: State-based, non-state, and one-sided violence. Strong source for triangulation against ACLED.

sources.name: Varieties of Democracy
sources.notes: 3,500+ expert raters scoring civil liberties, electoral integrity, freedom from torture and political killing back to 1789. Best for long time-series.

sources.name: Human Rights Measurement Initiative
sources.notes: Country-level scores on civil/political and economic/social rights. Comparable, methodology fully published. Closest thing to a global rights scorecard.

sources.name: UN Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights
sources.notes: Universal Human Rights Index + Universal Periodic Review recommendations. Verified casualty data in conflict zones (Ukraine, Gaza, Syria).

sources.name: Human Rights Watch
sources.license: Fair use for aggregation; attribution required
sources.notes: Investigative reporting on specific incidents. LLM extraction pulls (country, date, event_type, actor, casualties) from narrative reports.

sources.name: Amnesty International
sources.license: Fair use for aggregation; attribution required
sources.notes: Country reports + urgent actions. Pairs with HRW for cross-confirmation of single-source events.

sources.name: US State Department Country Reports on Human Rights Practices
sources.notes: Congressionally mandated. Useful for cross-reference; bias must be disclosed in record provenance. Notably does not cover US conduct.

sources.name: Stockholm International Peace Research Institute
sources.notes: Authoritative arms transfer database. Structural harm dataset — who is arming whom.

sources.name: Citizen Lab
sources.notes: Forensic confirmation of spyware deployments (Pegasus, Predator). Structural-harm coverage that traditional rights bodies miss.

sources.name: Environmental Justice Atlas
sources.notes: 4,000+ documented cases of environmental harm linked to industrial/extractive activity. The category most other ledgers omit entirely.

sources.name: USHMM Simon-Skjodt Center & Holocaust Encyclopedia
sources.notes: Risk assessments + historical encyclopedia coverage. Strong on early-warning signals for ongoing risk.

sources.name: Wikipedia: List of Genocides + Genocides in History
sources.notes: Most complete cross-cultural historical index in one place. Every claim cites underlying scholarship; we follow the citations rather than trusting the index directly.

sources.name: International Criminal Court
sources.notes: Adjudicated record. The 'verified' tier for the highest-stakes claims.

sources.name: GDELT Project
sources.notes: Massive real-time media event database. Noisy — used only for early-warning signal, never as primary source. Records flagged 'gdelt-only' are explicitly low-confidence.

ingestion_principles: Every record retains its source ID and original URL.
Records are never silently merged; they are linked into incidents with explicit confidence scores.
Disagreement between sources is shown, not hidden.
Single-source records are tagged 'single-source' rather than suppressed.
Bias of the source (e.g. State Dept on US adversaries) is part of the record metadata.
Every ingestion cycle writes a changelog entry to the public corrections log.
Same lens applies to all states. No category is exempt by virtue of geopolitical alignment.
